perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed indefinitely, regardless of any notice of non-renewal.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the agreement's renewal. Originally, the agreement automatically renewed for *one* additional term unless notice was given. Now, the agreement renews *indefinitely*, regardless of notice. This contradicts the initial condition that allowed for non-renewal, creating uncertainty about the actual term and renewal process. This directly contradicts the initial clause that mentions a potential non-renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed indefinitely, regardless of any notice of non-renewal. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement renews 'indefinitely, regardless of any notice of non-renewal.' The contradicted text states the same thing, but includes the clause: 'This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.' This directly contradicts the 'indefinitely, regardless of any notice of non-renewal' clause because it introduces the possibility of termination with notice, creating a significant inconsistency in the contract's renewal and termination terms. The change stating that the agreement renews indefinitely regardless of notice introduces the direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be unilaterally decided by COMPANY regardless of any prior agreement, with the specific amount being at COMPANY's sole discretion, payable at COMPANY's convenience.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the determination of the lead-finder fee. The original clause stated the fee would be 'mutually discussed' within a range of 10-20%. The changed clause states the fee will be 'unilaterally decided by COMPANY' at its 'sole discretion'. This contradicts the previous agreement on how the fee is determined, creating uncertainty about Bravatek's compensation. It also makes the range that has been previously agreed upon useless because the fee is ultimately determined by one party. The phrase 'prior agreement' indicates this contradiction more explicitly. The whole sentence is now contradicted due to this insertion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. 2 For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be unilaterally decided by COMPANY regardless of any prior agreement, with the specific amount being at COMPANY's sole discretion, payable at COMPANY's convenience. 5) Confidentiality "Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text specified a lead-finder fee to be 'mutually discussed' within a 10-20% range. The changed text makes the fee 'unilaterally decided' by COMPANY, regardless of any prior agreement. This directly contradicts the notion of mutual agreement and introduces a clear inconsistency in how the fee is determined, making the agreement unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may only be terminated by COMPANY upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that *either* party can terminate the agreement with 90 days' notice. The changed text restricts this right, stating that *only COMPANY* can terminate the agreement. This directly contradicts the originally agreed-upon terms, impacting the rights and obligations of Bravatek. Now Bravatek is binded, by force, to an agreement they don't have freedom to terminate, creating a clear in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed indefinitely, regardless of any notice of non-renewal. This Agreement may only be terminated by COMPANY upon ninety (90) days written notice. 3 8) Notices Any notices required under this Agreement shall be delivered to: Bravatek Technologies, Inc. 2028 E. Ben White Blvd., Unit #240-2835 Austin, Texas 78741 Sibannac, Inc. 2122 E Highland Avenue, STE 425 Phoenix, AZ 85016
perturbation.justification: The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement. The changed text restricts this right solely to COMPANY. The contradicted text states the agreement 'may only be terminated by COMPANY'. While the contradicted text echoes the changed text, the alteration from the original 'either party' introduces a contradiction to what Bravatek initially agreed to. Now Bravatek is forced into an agreement it cannot terminate, creating a legally significant in-text contradiction.
